title: Will Trump make 1 trips to Mar-a-Lago as President in Jun 2026?

current.lean_note: 50pt gap exceeds the hard ceiling (20pt) — beyond this divergence from a liquid market, even high confidence is treated as model error, not edge
current.rationale_summary: The evidence provided does not directly address whether Trump will make 1 trip to Mar-a-Lago in June 2026. The only relevant information is about Trump's son potentially buying waterfront land in West Palm, which is unrelated to the number of trips to Mar-a-Lago. key_drivers: No direct evidence about Trump's travel plans to Mar-a-Lago in June 2026
Lack of historical data on Trump's trips to Mar-a-Lago as President
reference_classes: Presidential travel patterns to private residences
Public statements or records about Trump's activities at Mar-a-Lago
research_refs: Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 10%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: A single personal-use Florida visit in the Jun 18-30 window is possible but unlikely given seasonal closure and Iran/G7 schedule.

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 13%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: Coherence with P(0 trips)~0.82: residual mass for exactly 1 trip is modest. Summer/war suppress Florida travel.

High confidence not justified by thin evidence (single narrow-window scenario)
Base-rate neglect: historical data shows zero trips through June 23, yet probability assumes plausibility without concrete triggers
history.rationale_summary: Base rate of off-season one-off Mar-a-Lago trips is low, but a single unscheduled trip during June 24-30 remains plausible if an unexpected Florida event occurs.
